class.name: GKTurnBasedMatchmakerViewController
class.description: Wrapper around [code skip-lint]GKTurnBasedMatchmakerViewController[/code]. Use [method create_controller] when you want to keep the controller instance and listen to its signals, or [method request_match] for a one-shot request/callback flow.

method.name: create_controller
method.description: Creates a controller for the provided [GKMatchRequest]. Call [method present] to show it and connect to signals such as [signal did_find_match] and [signal failed_with_error].

method.name: request_match
method.description: Shows turn-based matchmaking UI and returns the result through [code skip-lint]callback[/code]. The callback receives [code](GKTurnBasedMatch match, Variant error)[/code]. On failure or cancel, [code]match[/code] is [code]null[/code] and [code]error[/code] contains a [code skip-lint]GKError[/code].

members.member: Controls how players are matched. See [enum MatchmakingMode].
If [code]true[/code], the UI can show the player's existing turn-based matches.

signal.name: cancelled
signal.description: Emitted when the matchmaking UI is canceled.

signal.name: did_find_match
signal.description: Emitted when a turn-based match is found.

signal.name: failed_with_error
signal.description: Emitted when matchmaking fails with an error message.

signal.name: player_quit_for_match
signal.description: Emitted when a player quits the specified turn-based match from the matchmaking UI flow.

constants.constant: Use Apple's default turn-based matchmaking behavior.
